perturbation.original_text: 2.13 Delivery or Completion. "Delivery" or "Completion" or any similar terms mean Vendor's obligation to provide Material and Services that strictly conform to the Specifications, including, without limitation, Location Acceptance of the Cell Sites described in the Build Addendum. Vendor completes Delivery of Material or Completion of Services: (i) in the case of any Cell Site (and all Material and Services incorporated therein or related thereto) upon Location Acceptance, and (ii) in the case of delivery of Material or Services that are required to be delivered prior to or following Location Acceptance, (A) upon AT&T's possession of the Material if Vendor is not required to provide additional Services, such as installation, (B) upon completing such additional Services, if Vendor is required to provide such Services in connection with providing Material, or (C) for Services, upon completing the provision of Services. Notwithstanding the above, Delivery and Completion shall not be deemed to have occurred until Vendor causes the Material and Services to strictly conform to the Specifications and in the case of any Cell Site, Location Acceptance has occurred.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.13 Delivery or Completion. “Delivery” or “Completion” or any similar terms mean Vendor’s *endeavor* to provide Material and Services that conform to the Specifications, including, without limitation, Location Acceptance of the Cell Sites described in the Build Addendum. Vendor *attempts to complete* Delivery of Material or Completion of Services: (i) in the case of any Cell Site (and all Material and Services incorporated therein or related thereto) upon Location Acceptance, and (ii) in the case of delivery of Material or Services that are required to be delivered prior to or following Location Acceptance, (A) upon AT&T's possession of the Material if Vendor is not required to provide additional Services, such as installation, (B) upon completing such additional Services, if Vendor is required to provide such Services in connection with providing Material, or (C) for Services, upon attempting to complete the provision of Services. Notwithstanding the above, Delivery and Completion shall be deemed to have occurred when Vendor *tries to* cause the Material and Services to conform to the Specifications and in the case of any Cell Site, Location Acceptance has occurred.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Vendor's obligation to provide Material and Services that strictly conform' with 'Vendor’s endeavor to provide Material and Services that conform' and changing 'Vendor completes' to 'Vendor attempts to complete', the contract now implies that only an effort to adhere to specifications is required, not actual adherence. This contradicts the clause 'Delivery and Completion shall not be deemed to have occurred until Vendor causes the Material and Services to strictly conform to the Specifications' and thus creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.13 Delivery or Completion. “Delivery” or “Completion” or any similar terms mean Vendor’s endeavor to provide Material and Services that conform to the Specifications, including, without limitation, Location Acceptance of the Cell Sites described in the Build Addendum. Vendor attempts to complete Delivery of Material or Completion of Services: (i) in the case of any Cell Site (and all Material and Services incorporated therein or related thereto) upon Location Acceptance, and (ii) in the case of delivery of Material or Services that are required to be delivered prior to or following Location Acceptance, (A) upon AT&T's possession of the Material if Vendor is not required to provide additional Services, such as installation, (B) upon completing such additional Services, if Vendor is required to provide such Services in connection with providing Material, or (C) for Services, upon attempting to complete the provision of Services. Notwithstanding the above, Delivery and Completion shall be deemed to have occurred when Vendor tries to cause the Material and Services to conform to the Specifications and in the case of any Cell Site, Location Acceptance has occurred.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the requirement for 'strict conformance' with an 'endeavor to conform' and 'attempts to complete'. This directly contradicts the original requirement that 'Delivery and Completion shall not be deemed to have occurred until Vendor causes the Material and Services to strictly conform to the Specifications'. The original text mandates strict adherence, while the modified text suggests that a mere attempt or endeavor is sufficient. This creates a clear and impactful contradiction regarding the vendor's obligation and the conditions for Delivery and Completion.

perturbation.original_text: 2.38 Specifications. "Specifications" means (i) all of the Build Out Specifications set forth in the Build Addendum (and all schedules thereto, except as expressly waived by AT&T in writing), (ii) all of the specifications set forth in the Maintenance Addendum (and all schedules thereto) with respect to the Services contemplated therein, and (iii) all applicable OEM specifications.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.38 Specifications. "Specifications" means (i) *most* of the Build Out Specifications set forth in the Build Addendum (and all schedules thereto, except as expressly waived by AT&T in writing), (ii) all of the specifications set forth in the Maintenance Addendum (and all schedules thereto) with respect to the Services contemplated therein, and (iii) all applicable OEM specifications *when feasible*.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of “Specifications” to include only 'most' of the Build Out Specifications and all applicable OEM specifications 'when feasible', the contract now creates uncertainty about which specifications are actually binding. This change contradicts earlier sections of the agreement that require 'strict conformance with the Specifications'. This also undermines the original definition found in section 2.38, in another context.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.10 Delivery, Performance and Acceptance (a) Vendor acknowledges that AT&T is responsible for the deployment of the FirstNet Nationwide Public Safety Broadband Network (the "NPSBN") in accordance with the terms of AT&T's agreement with the FirstNet Authority and that AT&T has provided certain terms of such agreement to Vendor but has not provided Vendor with a copy of such agreement with the FirstNet Authority. Therefore, Vendor understands generally that AT&T's business requires prompt Delivery of Material and provision of Services by the specified Delivery Dates. Furthermore, the Parties agree that dates for Delivery of Material and Services are firm subject to any Excusable Delay, time is of the essence, and, subject to any Excusable Delay, Vendor will complete such Delivery in strict conformance with the Specifications.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity into the definition of "Specifications" by stating that only 'most' of the Build Out Specifications are included and OEM specifications are applicable only 'when feasible'. This directly contradicts section 3.10, which requires 'strict conformance with the Specifications'. The original agreement was clear on the binding nature of the specifications, but the modified text creates uncertainty about which specifications must be strictly followed, thus weakening and contradicting the original intent and creating a strong in-text contradiction. According to the Perturbation Definitions, Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction states that it 'occur when key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear', the change falls into this category.
